title: FIFA World Cup breaks 32-year Fox and Telemundo record
description: Soccer is perhaps the most popular sport in the world, even if it is not the biggest in the United States of America. The NFL, NBA, etc, are generally more popular than ‘football’, but the 2026 World Cup meant a lot more eyes were on soccer across America this summer.
fullText:
Soccer is perhaps the most popular sport in the world, even if it is not the biggest in the United States of America.

The NFL, NBA, etc, are generally more popular than ‘football’, but the 2026 World Cup meant a lot more eyes were on soccer across America this summer.

In turn, it is perhaps not surprising to see the tournament breaking viewership records on Fox and Telemundo.

The USMNT were knocked out of the 2026 World Cup in the Round of 16 after losing 4-1 to Belgium.

Despite the early exit, it seems like many were still heavily invested in the competition all the way up to the final.

This was made clear after Fox’s Michael Mulvihill tweeted that the final between Spain and Argentina set viewership records on the network.

He stated: “The World Cup Final averaged 66.4 million viewers in the U.S. on FOX and Telemundo combined. 42.5 million on FOX. 23.9 million on Telemundo.

“Other than Super Bowls, this is the largest sports audience since the 1994 Winter Olympics.”

Soccer in America still has some way to go if it is to reach the heights of the Super Bowl, however.

The 2026 showpiece is reported to have had over 125 million viewers on Fox earlier this year, around double that of the World Cup.

Of course, that may have been different had the USMNT reached the final, but that was always going to be an unlikely scenario.

Still, it may lead to more people inside the USA starting to take soccer a bit more seriously, given there is clearly a decent audience to capitalise on.

title: NBC earns best Open Championship audience since 2022
description: American sports fans looking for an appetizer prior to Sunday’s World Cup final seemed to get one in the 154th Open Championship. NBC’s final-round coverage of the last men’s golf major of the year averaged 4.3 million viewers, up six percent from last year’s victory from Scottie Scheffler at Royal Portrush (4.1 million viewers). Ryan…
fullText:
American sports fans looking for an appetizer prior to Sunday’s World Cup final seemed to get one in the 154th Open Championship.

NBC’s final-round coverage of the last men’s golf major of the year averaged 4.3 million viewers, up six percent from last year’s victory from Scottie Scheffler at Royal Portrush (4.1 million viewers). Ryan Fox’s dramatic win capped with a birdie on the 72nd hole peaked at 6.1 million viewers during the 1:15 p.m. ET quarter-hour.

As usual, the Open Championship was the least-watched of the four majors due to a less favorable time zone for the American audience. NBC’s final-round window began at 9 a.m. and went through 2 p.m. ET, whereas final-round coverage of the Masters, for instance, begins at 2 p.m. and typically runs until about 7 p.m. ET. Those Sunday afternoon hours are prime viewing times for the U.S. audience. Sunday morning? Not so much.

As Sports Business Journal’s Josh Carpenter lays out, June’s U.S. Open averaged 5.5 million viewers in the final round, the PGA Championship 5.7 million viewers in May, and the Masters 13.9 million viewers in April.

NBC: 4.3 million viewers for the final round of @TheOpen, its best since 2022, though with the addition of Big Data, fair to wonder if last year (4.1M) would have outdrawn it.

Major viewership this year (final rounds)

Masters: 13.9M
 PGA: 5.7M
 U.S. Open: 5.5M
 Open: 4.3M pic.twitter.com/1jvOdTBxTz

— Josh Carpenter (@JoshACarpenter) July 21, 2026

Still, holding an audience of 4.3 million viewers across a five-hour window early on Sunday is a solid result for NBC. It should be noted, this is the first Open Championship to be measured using Nielsen’s new Big Data + Panel standard, and the six percent year-over-year increase falls within the range that could solely be explained by the changes.

Saturday saw a larger year-over-year bump, however. Third-round coverage on NBC and Peacock averaged 3.7 million viewers, up 16 percent versus last year (3.2 million viewers) and good for the most-watched third round since 2018.

The post NBC earns best Open Championship audience since 2022 appeared first on Awful Announcing.

title: Rantala becomes third Leicester player to join Everton
description: Everton confirm the signing of Finland international Jutta Rantala from Leicester City.
fullText:
Everton have confirmed the signing of Finland forward Jutta Rantala from Leicester City.

Leicester have said, external Rantala had a year remaining on her contract and it is understood Everton have activated an undisclosed release clause to sign the 26-year-old.

BBC Sport reported on Tuesday that the clubs - who have already agreed two previous deals this summer - had reached an agreement for her transfer.

France forward Noemie Mouchon joined Everton from Leicester on a free last week while Wales striker Hannah Cain returned to Merseyside from the Foxes two days later.

Rantala scored 13 goals in 48 appearances since joining Leicester from Swedish club Vittsjo GIK in 2023.

She was Leicester's player of the season and top-scorer in 2023-24 but suffered a knee injury in the following campaign.

Rantala has scored 10 goals in 36 caps for Finland and was part of their squad at Euro 2025.

She is the sixth player to depart Leicester this summer after they were relegated from the Women’s Super League in June.

Everton are looking to build on last season's eighth-place finish in the WSL following the departure of manager Brian Sorensen.

Scott Phelan was named his permanent successor this summer and they have made six signings so far, including the trio from Leicester.

title: Meet the teenager tasked with filling Alexia Putellas’ boots at Barcelona
description: “They’re the best in the world; they prove it every year by playing so well. fullText:
“They’re the best in the world; they prove it every year by playing so well. I mean … we play very well,” Clara Serrajordi said with a laugh after reaching the Champions League final at Camp Nou last May, beating Bayern Munich in last season’s semi-finals.

The 18-year-old midfielder’s confusion with “they” and “we” makes sense. Until last season, she was on the outside looking in.

Serrajordi was used to watching the first team from her home at La Masia, the famed Barcelona youth academy known for fostering top talent in the club’s pipeline. The team’s six previous Champions League final appearances were games she only saw from afar. Less than a decade ago, she waited anxiously in the stands to see if the team’s all-time leading goalscorer, Alexia Putellas, would take a photo with her.

But in the span of a year, her daydreams became a reality.

She has gone from taking photos with Putellas to forming the starting midfield trio alongside her and Patri Guijarro — both of whom she calls her big sisters. Together they won Liga F, Copa de la Reina, the Spanish Super Cup and the Champions League.

Next season, she will face yet another tall task, becoming her idol’s successor after Putellas’ recent move to England’s London City Lionesses. It requires filling the shoes of a player who spent 14 years at Barcelona, won 38 trophies and is the club’s joint-second highest all-time scorer, alongside Cesar Rodriguez on 232 goals, trailing only Lionel Messi (672).

Barcelona knew players like Putellas, Mapi Leon and Ona Batlle would leave and wanted to protect itself from yet another offseason of departures. However, without the financial resources to sign players without restraint, Barcelona are limited by an economic crisis affecting the entire club.

Instead, they looked to players coming through from La Masia. Players like Serrajordi.

Serrajordi passed her first test, replacing Bonmati, the current Ballon d’Or holder, after she suffered a transverse fracture of her left fibula in November and was out until May. The youngster stepped up for her club and made her debut for Spain’s senior national team aged 17.

In the second leg of the Champions League semi-finals, she controlled both legs against Bayern Munich from the centre of midfield. After that 5-3 aggregate victory, at the age of 18 and 167 days, she became the youngest Barcelona player to start a Champions League final against OL Lyonnes in Oslo.

She played 42 matches in her first season with the senior team, starting 25, scoring twice and providing two assists. Her achievements make her a clear contender to succeed team-mate Vicky Lopez as the next Golden Girl, the annual award given to the best female under-21 football player in Europe.

Some within the club describe Serrajordi as a cross between Putellas and Guijarro. They even joke that she looks like a combination of the two. Though she started as an attacking midfielder, in recent years in Barcelona’s youth teams, she has played as a defensive midfielder.

Described as having a powerful physique, keen ball-winning skills and an ability to anticipate the next play. She possesses technical ability and excellent dribbling skills to break through opposition lines.

The tactical astuteness is not limited to the pitch. As a child, she used to compete in chess tournaments, but eventually gave it up due to the commitment. She even played chess against Barcelona men’s player Pedri for a commercial. The two share an agency, Leaderbrock Sports Group. Her father, upon seeing the pictures of the game, asked, “Did you beat Pedri at least?”

Despite still being in her teens, Serrajordi rarely checks social media to see what people are saying about her. Instead, she maintains her calm, taking everything that’s happened over the last year in her stride.

Others might have been overwhelmed by such a daunting challenge, but Serrajordi approached it with the positive, carefree attitude of a younger generation, similar to Lamine Yamal, Lopez or Pau Cubarsi.

When Serrajordi returned to Spain after winning her first Champions League title, she went back to La Masia, where she will continue to live until November of this year. However, replacing a player she looked up to is not just difficult on the pitch, but challenging in other ways.

When Putellas announced her departure, Serrajordi was one of the most emotional players at the farewell ceremony, despite having played alongside her for just one season.

“I would have liked to have made it (into the first team) sooner,” Serrajordi said in an interview with Diari Ara in May. “But I think she’s done everything she needed to do at Barça and has opened many doors for us.”

During the Champions League celebrations, before La Reina’s departure was public, Putellas was very active in supporting Serrajordi, including featuring her in a post-game, pre-party Instagram Live.

The day after returning from Oslo, Norway, Putellas used her time on the microphone during the public celebrations at Montjuïc to chant, “Serra, Serra.”

“We baked the cake and now they’ll eat it,” Putellas said, looking at her younger team-mates during her farewell.

“Becoming a new Alexia is impossible for me. There will never be anyone like her. That much is clear, and I don’t put that pressure on myself because I won’t be her,” Serrajordi said in an interview with Diari Ara.

Still, with her presence, the future of Barcelona’s midfield remains in safe hands.

This article originally appeared in The Athletic.

title: Posts falsely claim Lamine Yamal snubbed Donald Trump after World Cup win
description: Spain star Lamine Yamal did not avoid shaking Donald Trump's hand after the World Cup final, contrary to posts claiming the teenager snubbed the US president and speculating he may have done so because of US policy regarding Palestinians. The posts cited as proof a video of the interaction filmed from behind Yamal that partially obscures Trump, but AFP photos and footage of the post-match ceremony show the pair did shake hands.
fullText:
Spain star Lamine Yamal did not avoid shaking Donald Trump's hand after the World Cup final, contrary to posts claiming the teenager snubbed the US president and speculating he may have done so because of US policy regarding Palestinians. The posts cited as proof a video of the interaction filmed from behind Yamal that partially obscures Trump, but AFP photos and footage of the post-match ceremony show the pair did shake hands.

"Lamine Yamal did not shake hands with Trump; he avoided the US president," says the Bengali-language caption of a YouTube video shared on July 20, 2026.

The video, which has been viewed more than 334,000 times, appears to show Spain's 19-year-old winger Lamine Yamal going to collect his World Cup winner's medal. He is first met by FIFA head Gianni Infantino before briefly stopping in front of US President Donald Trump, who drapes the medal over Yamal's head.

According to the video's Bengali-language voiceover, Yamal did not shake Trump's hand. It speculates this may be because of Trump's "foreign policy towards Palestinians".

The clip was also shared in similar YouTube and Facebook posts after Spain's 1-0 win over Argentina in the World Cup final on July 19, with the US president then presenting the World Cup trophy to La Roja ( archived link ).

"Lamine Yamal did the right thing 100 percent," says a comment on one of the posts, while another says "Thanks Spain, and thanks Yamal."

There is strong support for the Palestinian people in Muslim-majority Bangladesh, and the government reaffirmed its "steadfast commitment to the legitimate rights of the Palestinian people and the establishment of an independent and sovereign State of Palestine" in July ( archived link ).

Yamal has also expressed support for the Palestinian cause in the past, most recently during his club side Barcelona's title celebrations in May when he waved a Palestinian flag during an open-top bus parade ( archived link ).

Trump, meanwhile, has firmly backed Israel despite growing international outrage over its offensive in the Palestinian territory of Gaza, in response to the October 7, 2023 attacks carried out by Gaza-based militants Hamas ( archived link ).

There was an international  backlash when Trump said he wanted to take control of the Gaza strip and "permanently" resettle Palestinians in other countries ( archived link ).

The US president's Gaza peace plan -- which was endorsed by the UN Security Council in November 2025 -- does, however, include a path to a future Palestinian state ( archived link ).

The claims circulating on social media that Yamal avoided shaking Trump's hand are false, with the angle the falsely shared video appearing to have obscured the handshake.

Several  AFP photos , taken from a different angle to the falsely shared video, show  Yamal and Trump shaking hands as the teenager received his winner's medal.

Moreover, a  keyword search on Google led to similar footage shared on the verified YouTube channels of the White House and Fox Sports showing the pair shaking hands (archived  here and here ).

title: 'Thank you for all the unforgettable memories' - Anderson says goodbye
description: Elliot Anderson has posted an emotional goodbye to Nottingham Forest as he prepares to join Manchester City.  Earlier in July, City confirmed an agreement has been reached to sign Anderson, stating the "formalities of the move will be finalised upon his return to England" after the World Cup.  BBC Sport reported a deal had been put in place on 25 June.
fullText:
Elliot Anderson has posted an emotional goodbye to Nottingham Forest as he prepares to join Manchester City.

Earlier in July, City confirmed an agreement has been reached to sign Anderson, stating the "formalities of the move will be finalised upon his return to England" after the World Cup. BBC Sport reported a deal had been put in place on 25 June.

The 23-year-old made 92 appearances across two seasons at Forest after joining from boyhood club Newcastle in 2024, helping them qualify for Europe in 2024-25 and reach the Europa League semi-finals the following year.

The goodbye message posted by Anderson on social media read: "Forest family,

"Firstly I am so grateful for the love and support you have shown me over the last 2 years especially towards the end of this season. From the minute I walked through the door I fell in love with this club.

"I want to thank Mr Marinakas, the staff and all players for welcoming me and making my time here so enjoyable, this club gave me the opportunity to grow as a player and a person.

"Bringing the club European football again after 29 years is what all of you fans deserved and to be a helping hand in that is so special.

"Nottingham forest will always have a special place in my heart. Thank you for all the unforgettable memories."

title: Real Madrid schedule first preseason match under Jose Mourinho
description: Jose Mourinho has been training the available Real Madrid players for some days now, and even the first player who participated in the World Cup, Arda Guler, has also joined up with him at Valdebebas....
fullText:
Jose Mourinho has been training the available Real Madrid players for some days now, and even the first player who participated in the World Cup, Arda Guler, has also joined up with him at Valdebebas. The new manager has also called up several youth team players to train under him as well.

After Guler’s return, several other players are expected to join the Portuguese trainer in the coming few days. Federico Valverde, whose Uruguay side was also eliminated in the group stage, like Guler’s Turkiye, will be coming back soon, as will be Antonio Rudiger, whose German side was eliminated in the Round of 32.

However, it remains to be seen whether they will be ready and available for the first training match of the preseason, which is scheduled in only a few days’ time. As MARCA has reported, Jose Mourinho will play his first match with Real Madrid against Alcorcon, this Friday, 24 July.

This is going to be a training match, and not a friendly, and thus, will be played behind closed doors. Unlimited substitutions will be allowed, as this will be the first training match after the holidays for players, and the managers will also want to test several of their players and setups.

Alcorcon are also looking to debut in their new season kits, which are expected to be unveiled on Thursday, in this training match against Real Madrid. As is the case with Jose Mourinho, the Alcorcon manager, Fran Justo, will also be overseeing this game as the first of his stint on his team’s bench.

Although it is not going to be a competitive game, it will provide a good opportunity for several Real Madrid youngsters to showcase their talents to Jose Mourinho, and thus make a place for themselves in the coach’s plans for the upcoming season.

title: Wilson can bring something 'very special' to Leeds - Ampadu
description: Leeds United captain Ethan Ampadu believes new signing Harry Wilson can bring something "very special" to the team through his contributions in attacking areas.  Ampadu and Wilson are team-mates at international level for Wales and the midfielder was asked by BBC Radio Leeds about whether he helped convince the attacker to make the switch from Fulham to Elland Road.  "I don't think he needed my convincing to join," Ampadu said.
fullText:
Leeds United captain Ethan Ampadu believes new signing Harry Wilson can bring something "very special" to the team through his contributions in attacking areas.

Ampadu and Wilson are team-mates at international level for Wales and the midfielder was asked by BBC Radio Leeds about whether he helped convince the attacker to make the switch from Fulham to Elland Road.

"I don't think he needed my convincing to join," Ampadu said. "I think he knew what an amazing club this is, what a great time he can have here. I like to think my message or two might've aided him in that direction. I think whenever I've spoken to him in the past the Welsh lads on camp have spoken glowingly about Leeds.

"All the conversations he's had with the manager and club have gone in the right direction. Because I know him quite well and I know him on the pitch, I'm grateful that we've got him in.

"I think everyone saw more last year, I think maybe because he was coming to the end of his contract that gets people talking about you in a different way but his quality, his left foot, the way he works for the team, the way he works for himself, I think all Leeds fans are going to appreciate the hard work he does and the goals and assists is very special and what helps results."

Ampadu also touched on how the Leeds team will have to change this coming season after achieving their goal of staying in the Premier League.

"I think you always have to try to adapt," he said. "You always have got to lean on what worked and you've also got to adapt on what you can improve, so I think any way we can do that is going to be done.

"I think collectively as a team we want to improve no matter which way it is, that's why we have pre-season. It's one of the most demanding leagues in the world, so we know the physicality has got to be there again. We've got to try and outrun teams again and make sure we're hard to play against and then things adapt because you want to try and find ways to win."

title: Las Vegas Raiders owner Mark Davis weighs in on grass fields movement after the World Cup
description: Las Vegas Raiders owner Mark Davis has broken his silence on the demand for NFL teams to use grass fields on the back of this summer’s World Cup. The Allegiant Stadium was not one of the host venues for the United States at the World Cup, but it is one of the NFL stadiums that uses a natural grass playing surface.
fullText:
Las Vegas Raiders owner Mark Davis has broken his silence on the demand for NFL teams to use grass fields on the back of this summer’s World Cup.

The Allegiant Stadium was not one of the host venues for the United States at the World Cup, but it is one of the NFL stadiums that uses a natural grass playing surface.

There is an argument to be made that a natural surface is easier for Vegas to maintain given it is an indoor venue, which does not expose the grass to the elements.

However, Davis is one of the owners who believes playing on a grass field is largely beneficial for his players’ health and wellbeing.

This is not a view that is shared across the league, with numerous NFL players now protesting their thoughts after seeing grass pitches laid down for the World Cup soccer teams.

As the Dallas Cowboys have already ripped up their grass field at AT&T Stadium, Davis has issued a message on the importance of his players playing on a natural surface.

In recent days, Seattle Seahawks fans have urged their new owners to lay down a grass surface at Lumen Field.

It is proving the hottest topic of discussion across the league, as the players make their voices heard over social media.

“There was no way I was going to build the stadium or anything else to play on artificial turf,” said the 71-year-old.

“I just always felt that the safety issue, number one, and I grew up around football and the grass and mud and the smell of those uniforms after the game.”

The Raiders’ stadium cost just shy of $2 billion to build and Davis did not cost cut by going with an artificial playing surface for his players.

Pat McAfee has backed the NFL players’ protest for grass fields, with Davis also backing the notion it should be an investment from the owners towards their players’ safety.

Owners across the league are happy to pay out huge wages to their star players, but there is a clear hesitation from some of them to splash out on installing and maintaining a grass field.

Witnessing the same stadiums across the nation suddenly install grass for the World Cup will have come as a slap in the face for NFL players across the league.

This is why the movement is picking up steam ahead of the 2026 season getting underway.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo Ranks Fourth In Post-World Cup Instagram Gains, Adding 11.4M Followers
description: Cristiano Ronaldo emerged as one of the biggest social media winners of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, finishing fourth among The Cristiano Ronaldo Ranks Fourth In Post-World Cup Instagram Gains, Adding 11.4M Followers first appeared on The Blast
fullText:
Cristiano Ronaldo emerged as one of the biggest social media winners of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, finishing fourth among the tournament’s top Instagram gainers. The Portugal star added 11.4 million followers despite his country failing to reach the latter stages.

Ronaldo also sparked controversy after his verified account appeared to endorse a post accusing FIFA of favoring Argentina at the competition.

Overall, Norway striker Erling Haaland led all other players at the World Cup in growth of IG numbers, as he added a whopping 32.1 million followers to his audience.

Cristiano Ronaldo may already be the most-followed athlete on Instagram, but the Portuguese icon still proved the FIFA World Cup could grow even the biggest global brands.

At the just-concluded global soccer showpiece, Ronaldo finished fourth among the tournament’s biggest Instagram gainers. According to a Sportico analysis of social media growth during the World Cup, the five-time Ballon d’Or winner added 11.4 million followers during the 38-day competition to climb from 665.6 million to a staggering 677 million.

This boost represented a relatively modest 1.71% increase compared to that of the tournament’s breakout stars, but it was still enough to place Ronaldo behind only Erling Haaland, Vozinha, and Jude Bellingham in total followers gained after the tournament.

The continued social media dominance by the Al-Nassr forward stands out even more when you consider Portugal failed to reach the latter stages.

Ronaldo may have added another 11.4 million followers, but he was comfortably outpaced by Haaland, who topped the rankings after gaining 32.1 million during the tournament.

The Norway striker’s following reportedly surged from 40.6 million to 72.7 million, representing an increase of roughly 80%. His quirky and playful posts also helped introduce his personality to a wider American audience as Norway reached the quarterfinals.

Days after being knocked out of the tournament by England, Haaland was spotted partying with Michele Morrone at a Dolce & Gabbana event in Italy, as reported by The Blast.

On the pitch, Haaland finished the tournament with seven goals, helping Norway reach the quarterfinals in their first World Cup appearance since 1998.

The towering forward also won over fans across the United States with his personality and off-field adventures. He attended Game 5 of the NHL Stanley Cup Final in North Carolina before Norway’s tournament opener, cheered on the Carolina Hurricanes, and explored local attractions during breaks in the schedule.

Haaland even went viral after buying a taxidermied raccoon from a Texas souvenir shop and joking on social media that “It followed me home.” Those memorable moments, combined with his performances for Norway, would have undoubtedly helped him on his way to becoming the tournament’s biggest Instagram winner.

Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha was arguably the list’s biggest surprise, finishing second with 29.4 million new followers after beginning the tournament with an audience of only around 50,000. England star Jude Bellingham placed third with 12 million, narrowly ahead of Ronaldo.

Spain sensation Lamine Yamal followed Ronaldo in fifth place after gaining 11 million followers, while Neymar and Lionel Messi added 7.7 million and 7.5 million, respectively. Mexico youngster Gilberto Mora, France’s Michael Olise, and Brazilian forward Endrick completed the top 10 with gains ranging from 5.6 million to 6.6 million.

One particularly notable omission was Kylian Mbappé, who failed to crack the top 10 despite France’s run to the semifinals. Brazil, meanwhile, was the only country to place two players on the list, with Neymar and Endrick both enjoying sizeable boosts.

Ronaldo also found himself at the center of an off-field controversy after fans noticed his verified Instagram account had liked a video alleging FIFA helped Lionel Messi’s Argentina reach the 2026 World Cup final.

The post, which featured comments from Spanish journalist Pilar Rodríguez Losantos accusing football’s governing body of favoring the defending champions, quickly went viral and reignited debate over Argentina’s run to the title match.

Screenshots of the would-be like spread rapidly across social media before it later disappeared, though the Portuguese superstar has not publicly addressed the incident or clarified whether the interaction was intentional.

title: Adversity to history - Agyarko's world title dream
description: Belfast's Caoimhin Agyarko's dream is to become Ireland's first black professional boxing champion and he can reach his goal when he challenges Josh Kelly for the IBF light-middleweight title on Saturday.
fullText:
"I will become Ireland's first black world champion."

From early in Caoimhin Agyarko's career, the above was pinned to the top of his X account and while the profile is no more, the objective remains.

On Saturday, Belfast's Agyarko gets the opportunity he has craved since first lacing up the gloves when he faces IBF light-middleweight champion Josh Kelly in Saudi Arabia.

The bout has landed on the undercard of Anthony Joshua's ring return against Kristian Prenga in Jeddah.

Another Belfast man, Tommy McCarthy, made history as Ireland's first black European champion with victory over Bilal Laggoune in 2020, but Agyarko wants to break further ground.

"I'm 30 this year, started boxing at seven years old, and have had the dream of becoming the first black Irish world champion," Agyarko tells BBC Sport NI.

"I've already made history by becoming the first black Irishman to fight for a world title but that is not my plan.

"My plan is to become the first black Irish world champion and lead the way for the next generation of fighters and people from different backgrounds."

Born in London to an Irish mother and Ghanaian father, Agyarko spent his first seven years in England before relocating to the Turf Lodge area of west Belfast.

It was the nearby Holy Trinity club - a staple of the city's boxing fabric - where the love of the sweet science was forged.

As an amateur Agyarko won Irish and Ulster honours. He switched to the professional ranks in 2018 and racked up the wins before his progress was stalled by injury.

"It's been 22 years of hard work, dedication, setbacks, ups and downs, disappointments and big wins. But none of that matters - it's all on Saturday and this is everything I've dreamt of since I was a kid," Agyarko said.

"I'm a regular kid from west Belfast, from an Irish-Ghanian background. I'm nothing special, but just want to leave a legacy the next generation can aspire to."

A 2023 victory over Troy Williamson should have been Agyarko's springboard to bigger fights but it proved little more than a prelude to a frustrating 12 months with just one low-key outing.

However, quickfire wins in spring 2025 led to a Windsor Park meeting with Ishmael Davis, edging a split decision verdict having pulled himself off the canvas and suffering a badly swollen eye.

In April he was in Las Vegas, preparing to weigh-in for a final eliminator against Brandon Adams when the American collapsed and the fight was off.

Despite the IBF ordering another final eliminator against former champion Bakhram Murtazaliev, a deal was struck with Kelly's team and they meet on Saturday.

Those ups and downs are not exclusive to his career in the ring, as in May 2017, a then 20-year-old Agyarko was tasked with a different fight.

Agyarko was slashed down the side of his face after an unprovoked attack in Belfast city centre which left him requiring emergency surgery and fighting for life.

"Things have happened for a reason, in and out of the ring: setbacks after setbacks in boxing and in life," Agyarko said.

"I'm used to this and the one thing I've never let any of it do was break me. I've been very resilient over the past 10 years since the stabbing, but my mindset now is 'these things happen', so what can I do to keep pushing forward instead of dwelling on a situation that is out of my hands?"

Agyarko may have reached the world stage but he has not forgotten his roots.

Holy Trinity helped mould him as a fighter and a man.

"[It's] the place that has given me everything and the reason why I am where I am in life," Agyarko added, when talking about the gym.

Club coach Michael Hawkins Jr and former world title challenger Stephen Smith will be by Agyarko's side in Saudi Arabia, while Michael Hawkins Sr remains on hand to offer advice.

It is a club that has produced two-time super-featherweight world champion Anthony Cacace and former welterweight world champion Lewis Crocker.

Indeed, there is a little bit of symmetry as Cacace won his first title on a huge heavyweight undercard in Saudi Arabia when he dethroned Joe Cordina on the first Oleksandr Usyk-Tyson Fury clash in 2024.

"There's big inspiration from Cacace, another Holy Trinity man who went to Saudi and I'm about to do the same for an IBF world title," Agyarko said.

"You've also got Lewis Crocker who won an IBF world title and now it's my turn, so it feels like it's written in the stars and this is the path I'm meant to take.

"Even look back at Carl Frampton and his first world title was for the IBF, Ryan Burnett's was an IBF, so this is now my time."

Agyarko is under no illusions as to the magnitude of the task that stands in front of him this weekend.

Kelly, 32, represented Team GB at the 2016 Rio Olympics and has won 18 of his 20 fights, losing one and drawing one.

He started fast when beating Murtazaliev for the title in February, knocking the Russian down in round four, but had to come through a late rally when dropped in the ninth.

"It's not just fitness, but explosiveness when I'm putting my punches together," Agyarko said.

"Not throwing singles, I think he will struggle to deal with it, so I will need to let my hands go have the power, speed and footwork.

"Some have said we have similar styles in that we are both reactive fighters, that I can use my head movement and footwork to get me out of trouble.

"I'm not going to play that game with him. I'm going to steamroll him and I'm looking forward to it."
